zh.lifeSummary: 當William Ham於1594年出生在英國戴文普萊姆斯時，他的父親Richard Ham24歲，母親Matilda24歲。他於1622年11月22日與Honor Stephens在英國英格蘭戴文普萊姆斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1673年1月26日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆波斯姆斯享年79歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆波斯姆斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Ham于1594年出生在英国戴文普萊姆斯时，他的父亲Richard Ham24岁，母亲Matilda24岁。他于1622年11月22日与Honor Stephens在英国英格兰戴文普萊姆斯结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1673年1月26日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆波斯姆斯享年79岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆波斯姆斯。

nameMeanings.name: Ham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Young, Ok, Nam, Chul, Eun Hee, Eun Young, Hae Jung, Il Hwa.
English (mainly southwestern) and German: variant of   Hamm  .
Dutch: topographic name from ham ‘land in a river bend’, ‘enclosed meadow’, or a habitational name from any of several country houses and places called De(n) Ham. Compare Van Ham and Vanderham .

variants.name: Hamme
meanings: Flemish (Van Hamme): variant of Ham 2, particularly referring to Hamme, a place in East Flanders.
German and English: variant of   Hamm  .

variants.name: Hamm
meanings: English (London): topographic name for someone who lived at a place called from Middle English ham(me), hom(me) (Old English hamm), which meant ‘land in a river bend’, ‘land hemmed in by marshland’, ‘wet land hemmed in by higher ground’, ‘river meadow’, or ‘cultivated plot on the edge of woodland or moor’. The topographic term is found mainly in the South Midlands and southern England. There are many farmsteads with this name in Devon and Sussex, five more substantial settlements called Ham or Hamp in Somerset, as well as East and West Ham in Essex, and places called Ham in Gloucestershire, Hampshire, Kent, Surrey, and Wiltshire. This form of the surname is also comparatively frequent in Ireland.
German: topographic name for someone who lived on land in a river bend, Old High German ham (see 1 above).
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from any of numerous places called Hamm, mainly the city in Westphalia.

variants.name: Hahm
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Sung, Yong, Byung, Chang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jee, Jong, Jong Hun, Jong Hyun, Moon Hee, Jung Suk, Sang, Seok, Seung, Soon, Sungtae, Taeyong.
German: metonymic occupational name for a sealer of weights and measures, from Middle High German hāme ‘(standard) measure’.

variants.name: Hams
meanings: English: variant of Ham with plural -s or post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: Massachusetts Colony Gains Control of New Hampshire
historicEvents.description: In 1641, Massachusetts gained control of New Hampshire until 1679 when New Hampshire became a separate royal colony.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When William Ham was born in 1594, in Plymouth, Devon, England, his father, Richard Ham, was 24 and his mother, Matilda, was 24. He married Honor Stephens on 22 November 1622, in Plymouth, Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 26 January 1673, in Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, at the age of 79, and was buried in Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Ham est né en 1594, à Plymouth, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Richard Ham, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Matilda, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Honor Stephens le 22 novembre 1622, à Plymouth, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 26 janvier 1673, à Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 79 ans, et a été enterré à Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, Amérique coloniale britannique.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Ham è nato il nel 1594 nel Plymouth, Devon, England, il suo padre, Richard Ham, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Matilda, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Honor Stephens il 22 novembre 1622, nel Plymouth, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 26 gennaio 1673, nel Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, all'età di 79 anni, e fu sepolto a Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: William Hamが1594年にイギリスデボンプリモウスで生まれた時，父Richard Hamは24歳，母Matildaは24歳であった。1622年11月22日にイギリスイギリスデボンプリモウスでHonor Stephensと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1673年1月26日にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアロクインガムポルトスモウスで79歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアロクインガムポルトスモウスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Ham(이)가 1594년 영국 데폰 플림우스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Ham(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1622년 11월 22일 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 플림우스에서 Honor Stephens(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1673년 1월 26일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 록잉함 포츠모우스에서 79세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 록잉함 포츠모우스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Ham nasceu em 1594, em Plymouth, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Ham, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Matilda, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Honor Stephens em 22 de novembro de 1622, em Plymouth, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 26 de janeiro de 1673, em Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, com 79 anos, e foi sepultado em Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Ham родился в 1594 г. в местности плимоут, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Ham было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Matilda, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Honor Stephens 22 ноября 1622 г. в местности плимоут, девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 26 января 1673 г. в местности порцмоут, роккингхам, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 79 лет и был похоронен в местности порцмоут, роккингхам, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Ham nació en 1594, en Plymouth, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Ham, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Matilda, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Honor Stephens el 22 de noviembre de 1622, en Plymouth, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 26 de enero de 1673, en Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, a la edad de 79 años, y fue sepultado en Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America.
